user_query: What is a fun finger food dinner option for child

selected_nodes.name: Creamy Tomato Soup with Grilled Cheese Dippers
selected_nodes.short_description: A kid-friendly classic — velvety tomato soup served with crispy whole-wheat grilled cheese strips for dunking.
selected_nodes.meal_suggestion: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil, add canned crushed tomatoes and a splash of milk or cream, then blend until smooth. Season with basil, salt, and pepper. Serve in a bowl alongside whole-wheat grilled cheese cut into sticks for easy dipping. A drizzle of olive oil on top adds richness.
selected_nodes.nutrition_note: Tomatoes are rich in lycopene and vitamin C; whole-wheat bread provides fiber and complex carbs; cheddar adds calcium and protein. Milk contributes additional calcium. Keep cream or milk portions moderate to manage fat; using part-skim cheese or reduced-fat milk can lighten the dish while preserving flavor.
selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 2 > Child > Day 2 > Dinner > Creamy Tomato Soup with Grilled Cheese Dippers
selected_nodes.reasoning: Grilled cheese 'dippers' are a classic fun finger food specifically designed for dunking, making it highly relevant to the 'fun finger food' request for a child.

selected_nodes.name: Homemade Pizza Night — Garden Veg Combo
selected_nodes.short_description: Homemade garden-veggie pizza with whole-wheat crust, tomato sauce, mozzarella, mushrooms, and bell pepper—colorful and fiber-rich.
selected_nodes.meal_suggestion: Serve a personal 8–12 inch pizza with a side salad. For a lighter version, use part-skim mozzarella and low-sodium tomato sauce, and consider adding more vegetables or a lean protein if desired.
selected_nodes.nutrition_note: Balanced: whole-wheat crust (fiber) + mozzarella (protein/calcium) + veggies (vitamins/minerals). Carbs mainly from crust; monitor sodium from cheese/sauce; consider portion control and optional low-sodium ingredients or extra veggies for fiber.
selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 2 > Child > Day 1 > Dinner > Homemade Pizza Night — Garden Veg Combo
selected_nodes.reasoning: Pizza is a quintessential finger food for children, and the 'Homemade Pizza Night' description implies a fun, interactive meal.

selected_nodes.name: Homemade Pizza Night — Mild-Spice Wrap
selected_nodes.short_description: Mild pizza-inspired wrap with whole-wheat crust, tomato sauce, mozzarella, mushrooms, and bell pepper — a fiber-rich, veggie-forward dish with calcium and protein.
selected_nodes.meal_suggestion: Serve as a 1-wrap portion; pair with a large green salad and water to create a balanced meal.
selected_nodes.nutrition_note: Fiber from whole-wheat crust supports fullness; vegetables add vitamins, while mozzarella provides protein and calcium. Monitor sodium and saturated fat from cheese and sauce; for a lighter option, use part-skim mozzarella and a bit less cheese, and pair with a side salad to boost fiber.
selected_nodes.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 2 > Child > Day 7 > Dinner > Homemade Pizza Night — Mild-Spice Wrap
selected_nodes.reasoning: This is a pizza-inspired wrap, which is easily held and eaten with hands, fitting the finger food criteria.
